steps.text: Initiate dialogue through written correspondence announcing a regulatory action that may have Tribal implications.
Send copies of correspondence relating to NEPA or NHPA actions to the Tribal Historic Preservation Officer (THPO) or equivalent staff.
Describe the proposed regulatory action and identify the location and methods for the Tribe to provide comments or request additional information in the initial correspondence.
Include information on the environmental review and NHPA Section 106 processes and an invitation to participate in the correspondence related to NEPA or the NHPA.
Conduct a follow-up meeting with Tribal officials or representatives based on expressions of interest from individual Tribes.
Establish relationships with Tribal representatives through written correspondence, phone calls, or e-mails.
Track and record communications by maintaining an administrative contact log.
Conduct a formal government-to-government meeting with a mutually approved agenda.
Check with the program office to determine if specific guidance exists for the NHPA Section 106 process or evaluating historic and cultural resources.
Exchange information and ideas with the appropriate Tribal attendees.
Allow sufficient time for Tribal representatives to caucus among themselves.
Solicit the views of Tribal attendees on the proposed NRC project or action.
Reach an agreement on processes or procedures for maintaining communication and resolving disputes.
Allow Tribal meeting participants the opportunity to review the NRC’s draft summary.
Provide participants with copies of the transcript and opportunity to provide written corrections.
Seek clarification of issues and work to resolve differences when there is a dispute on a meeting summary.
Summarize topics discussed and commitments made after telephone calls or webinars.
Follow the general agency correspondence procedures for written correspondence sent to a Tribal representative.
Follow up with a telephone call to the Tribal contact if a reply is not received within the expected time period.
Participate in cross cultural awareness training and establish professional relationships with Tribal representatives.
Provide time for people to speak without interruption during meetings.
Contact the NMSS Federal, State, and Tribal Liaison Branch for assistance in arranging translators.
Address Tribal leaders by their official titles and names and invite them to address the meeting first.
Respect the Tribe’s customs when a Tribal representative opens a meeting with a prayer, song, or invocation.
Establish familiarity and continuity.
Keep the focus where it belongs.
Be adaptable by exhibiting flexibility, sensitivity, and empathy in Tribal relations.
Be patient. Take time to understand, communicate with, and work with the Tribes who have an interest in NRC activities.

preconditions.text: Before beginning outreach with Tribes, determine whether any treaties exist and familiarize with treaty history.
